description: Polish side panel components: add keyboard focus rings, replace raw checkbox with styled Switch, extract duplicated tool grouping logic, and add npm link to NpmPluginCard.

userStories.title: Add visible keyboard focus ring to the design system
userStories.description: Every interactive element in the side panel uses focus:outline-hidden with no replacement focus indicator. Add a consistent focus-visible ring as a design system convention in styles.css, then apply it to accordion triggers in PluginCard and BrowserToolsCard.
acceptanceCriteria: A .focus-ring CSS class is defined in styles.css with focus-visible outline using theme primary color
The focus ring uses outline-offset: -2px (inset) to avoid layout shifts
AccordionPrimitive.Trigger in PluginCard.tsx uses focus-ring instead of focus:outline-hidden
AccordionPrimitive.Trigger in BrowserToolsCard.tsx uses focus-ring instead of focus:outline-hidden
The focus ring is only visible on keyboard navigation (focus-visible), not on mouse click
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/styles.css (add focus ring class)
- platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/PluginCard.tsx (apply to trigger)
- platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/BrowserToolsCard.tsx (apply to trigger)

Context:
The retro design system uses `--radius: 0` (flat corners), `--border` for all borders, and `--primary: #ffdb33` as the accent color. The focus ring should match this aesthetic: flat, visible, using the primary color.

PluginCard.tsx line 179:
```tsx
<AccordionPrimitive.Trigger className="flex min-w-0 flex-1 cursor-pointer items-center gap-2 px-3 py-2 focus:outline-hidden [&[data-state=open]>svg]:rotate-180">
```

BrowserToolsCard.tsx line 143:
```tsx
<AccordionPrimitive.Trigger className="flex min-w-0 flex-1 cursor-pointer items-center gap-2 px-3 py-2 focus:outline-hidden [&[data-state=open]>svg.chevron]:rotate-180">
```

Changes:

1. In styles.css, after the `.hide-number-spinner` block (end of file), add:
```css
/* Keyboard focus ring — flat retro style using primary accent */
.focus-ring {
  outline: none;
}
.focus-ring:focus-visible {
  outline: 2px solid var(--primary);
  outline-offset: -2px;
}
```

Using `outline-offset: -2px` (inset) ensures the ring doesn't extend outside the card boundary. The flat outline with no border-radius matches `--radius: 0`.

2. In PluginCard.tsx line 179, replace `focus:outline-hidden` with `focus-ring`:
```tsx
<AccordionPrimitive.Trigger className="focus-ring flex min-w-0 flex-1 cursor-pointer items-center gap-2 px-3 py-2 [&[data-state=open]>svg]:rotate-180">
```

3. In BrowserToolsCard.tsx line 143, replace `focus:outline-hidden` with `focus-ring`:
```tsx
<AccordionPrimitive.Trigger className="focus-ring flex min-w-0 flex-1 cursor-pointer items-center gap-2 px-3 py-2 [&[data-state=open]>svg.chevron]:rotate-180">
```

Constraints:
- Do NOT change the `:root` or `.dark` theme variable values.
- Do NOT add focus-ring to Radix primitives that already get focus styling from the library (Select, Switch, Dialog buttons).
- Do NOT use `ring-*` Tailwind utilities — they produce rounded rings. Use `outline` for a flat rectangle.
- Do NOT use `box-shadow` for the focus ring — outlines don't affect layout while box-shadows do.
- The `outline-offset: -2px` is critical — a positive offset on accordion triggers would extend outside the card border.

userStories.title: Replace raw checkbox with Switch in ConfirmationDialog
userStories.description: The 'Always allow this tool' checkbox in the confirmation dialog is a raw <input type='checkbox'> — the only unstyled form element in the UI. Replace it with the retro Switch primitive for visual consistency.
acceptanceCriteria: The 'Always allow' control uses the retro Switch component instead of a raw checkbox
The label text 'Always allow this tool' and helper text 'Sets permission to Auto' are grouped together next to the Switch
Toggling the Switch sets the alwaysAllow state correctly
The layout uses flex with gap for proper alignment
userStories.notes:
Files: - platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/ConfirmationDialog.tsx (replace checkbox with Switch) Context: The current checkbox at ConfirmationDialog.tsx lines 79-83: ```tsx Sets permission to Auto ``` The Switch primitive at retro/Switch.tsx uses Radix with `checked`/`onCheckedChange` props: ```tsx const Switch = ({ className, ...props }: SwitchPrimitives.SwitchProps) => ( ); ``` Changes: 1. Import Switch from `./retro/Switch.js` 2. Replace lines 79-83 with: ```tsx
Always allow this tool

Sets permission to Auto

``` This groups the helper text with the label and replaces the raw checkbox with the themed Switch. Constraints: - Do NOT create a smaller Switch variant — the default h-6 w-11 fits the dialog body. - Do NOT change the dialog layout, button order, or prev/next navigation. - Do NOT modify the Switch primitive itself. - Remove the old `

userStories.title: Extract shared tool grouping logic from PluginCard and BrowserToolsCard
userStories.description: Both PluginCard.tsx (lines 140-163) and BrowserToolsCard.tsx (lines 117-138) contain identical tool grouping logic. Extract into a shared utility function.
acceptanceCriteria: A groupTools function is exported from a new tool-groups.ts file
PluginCard.tsx uses the shared function instead of inline grouping logic
BrowserToolsCard.tsx uses the shared function instead of inline grouping logic
The grouping behavior is identical to the current inline logic (first-seen order, 'Other' last)
The function is generic (works with any type that has an optional group field)

userStories.title: Add npm package link to NpmPluginCard
userStories.description: NpmPluginCard shows name, version, author, and description but provides no link to inspect the package on npm before installing. Convert the display name into a link to the npm package page.
acceptanceCriteria: The plugin display name is rendered as a link to https://www.npmjs.com/package/{fullPackageName}
The link opens in a new tab with target='_blank' and rel='noopener noreferrer'
The link shows underline on hover, using text-foreground color
Clicking the link does not trigger any card-level handlers
userStories.notes:
Files: - platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/NpmPluginCard.tsx (convert name to link) Context: NpmPluginCard.tsx lines 24-30: ```tsx
{displayName} {plugin.version}
{plugin.author}
``` Note: `plugin.author` is already displayed at line 31 (the audit was initially wrong about this). The only missing piece is the npm link. `displayName` is the short name (e.g., 'slack'), while `plugin.name` is the full npm package name (e.g., '@opentabs-dev/opentabs-plugin-slack'). The URL should use `plugin.name`. Changes: Convert the `` at line 26 to an `` element: ```tsx e.stopPropagation()}> {displayName} ``` The `hover:underline` is a clean, minimal affordance that fits the retro aesthetic. The `e.stopPropagation()` prevents any parent handlers from interfering. Constraints: - Do NOT add an external link icon — it would clutter the tight card layout. - Do NOT change the card's overall structure or other elements. - Use `plugin.name` (full package name) for the URL, `displayName` (short name) for the text.
